verdict: Paid lead engine is working — $25 per lead beats legal benchmarks by 3x. Scale carefully.
diagnosis: 154 leads at $3,847 spend means roughly $25 per lead, far below the $72–$190 range typical for legal services this year.

signals.headline: 154 leads from $3,847 spend
signals.detail: Cost per lead landed near $25 this period — strong for a consumer law firm running Meta lead ads.

signals.headline: Click-through rate hit 4.2%
signals.detail: 2,488 clicks on 58,870 impressions doubles the legal category average. Creative is resonating with the targeted audience.

signals.headline: 897 landing page views, 144 pixel leads
signals.detail: Roughly 16% of page viewers convert. Healthy intake funnel — landing page is doing its job.

signals.headline: 135 calls from Google profile
signals.detail: Local presence is driving meaningful phone volume on top of paid. Two new 5-star reviews reinforce trust signals.

signals.headline: Instagram reach 3,569, only 28 followers
signals.detail: Paid is exposing the brand but organic following is not growing. Profile is leaking attention.

recommendations.title: Increase budget on the winning lead campaign
recommendations.detail: Cost per lead is roughly one-third of the category benchmark. Consider raising daily spend 25-40% on the active CROA campaign while creative still performs.

recommendations.title: Test a Spanish-language ad set
recommendations.detail: Organic data shows Spanish consumer-rights searches. A dedicated Spanish creative could meaningfully lower cost per lead and open a less crowded auction.

recommendations.title: Fix the Instagram profile leak
recommendations.detail: Reach hit 3,569 but only 28 followers. Worth testing a stronger bio, pinned reel explaining services, and a clear call to follow.
